training_phrases: when is the exam timetable out
when will the final exam be held
do you have the exam dates
where can I find the exam timetable
messages: I can help you find the exam timetable.

training_phrases: how do I register for courses
I need to register for next semester
messages: Here is information about course registration.

training_phrases: what time does the library close
is the library open on Sunday
messages: Here are the library opening hours.

training_phrases: how do I pay my fees
where can I pay my fees
messages: Here is how to pay your fees.

training_phrases: how do I request a transcript
how long does a transcript take
where can I get my transcript
messages: Here is how to request a transcript.

import_notes: Programmatic import: run `python backend/scripts/import_dialogflow_intents.py` after setting DIALOGFLOW_PROJECT_ID and GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS. The script is idempotent (create-or-update by display_name) and pulls answer text from faqs.json for each intent's fulfillment_text.
Dry-run: add --dry-run to print the plan without contacting GCP (works offline; cannot distinguish create vs update without listing the agent).
After import, set DIALOGFLOW_PROJECT_ID and GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS in .env so the runtime DialogflowClient switches off the keyword stub.

Keep this file in sync with knowledge_base/faqs.json — every intent here must have a matching faqs.json answer and vice versa. The importer (and tests/test_dialogflow_import.py::test_repo_kb_and_intents_are_in_sync) enforces this.
